question: Which districts in each division experienced the highest population growth rates from 1991 to 2022?

analysis.chart_type: Division-faceted heatmap (district vs growth rate, top-ranked highlighted)
analysis.design_rationale: A heatmap provides a visual matrix of growth rates for all districts, with divisions separated as facets to enable intra-division comparison. Highlighting the top district per division with a bold border or marker makes standout growth easily identified. This method leverages both categorical and quantitative data, and is scalable to all 64 districts without clutter.
analysis.target_insight: Shows distribution and ranking of growth rates for all districts, and visually emphasizes divisional leaders; also reveals high/low clusters in each division.

chart_encodings.other_aesthetics: Highlight cell of top district in each division (bold edge/marker), district names annotated

transformations: Calculate 'Population Growth Rate (1991-2022)' for each district as ((df['Population_2022'] - df['Population_1991']) / df['Population_1991']) * 100
Create wide-format (pivot) for heatmap: rows=Division, columns=Name, values=Growth Rate
Identify and mark top-growth district cell in each division
data_preparation_steps: Add new column 'Population Growth Rate (1991-2022)' to df
For each division, determine top-growth district
Pivot df to Division x Name matrix for heatmap
Prepare mask (or overlay) to highlight top-growth cells
suggestion.matplotlib_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib as mpl
df = pd.read_csv('csv_c_squared\268.csv')
df['Population Growth Rate (1991-2022)'] = ((df['Population_2022'] - df['Population_1991']) / df['Population_1991']) * 100
divisions = sorted(df['Division'].unique())
districts = sorted(df['Name'].unique())
pivot = df.pivot(index='Division', columns='Name', values='Population Growth Rate (1991-2022)')
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(18,6))
cmap = mpl.colormaps['YlGnBu']
im = ax.imshow(pivot.values, aspect='auto', cmap=cmap)
ax.set_yticks(np.arange(len(divisions)))
ax.set_yticklabels(divisions)
ax.set_xticks(np.arange(len(districts)))
ax.set_xticklabels(districts, rotation=90, fontsize=8)
for i, division in enumerate(divisions):
    division_data = df[df['Division'] == division]
    top_idx = division_data['Population Growth Rate (1991-2022)'].idxmax()
    top_district = df.loc[top_idx, 'Name']
    j = districts.index(top_district)
    rect = plt.Rectangle((j-0.5, i-0.5), 1, 1, edgecolor='red', linewidth=3, facecolor='none')
    ax.add_patch(rect)
    ax.text(j, i, top_district, ha='center', va='center', color='black', fontsize=9, fontweight='bold')
cbar = fig.colorbar(im, ax=ax, orientation='vertical')
cbar.set_label('Population Growth Rate (1991-2022) (%)')
ax.set_title('Population Growth Rate (1991-2022) for Districts in Each Division\nTop District Highlighted')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
